title: refactor(import): force usage of import and export ES6 keywords (+extension)
body:
These are three separate commits that should probably be reviewed in order because of the way it has been done (mostly scripted, a few manual modifications when needed).
I didn't do three PRs because of the potential conflicts that would have followed. I think squashing them will be the easiest solution.
### style(require): Force extension for local requires

If `require` content starts with a dot, add `.js` to the `require`.

``` sh
ag -l "require\([\"']\." | xargs -I {} sed -i -r "s#require\([\"'](\.[^\"']*/[^\.\"']*)[\"']\)#require('\1.js')#g" {} 
```

Few manual modifications afterwards.
### style(import): Use import over require at the root level

``` sh
# For `let xxx = require('xxx');`:
ag -l "^let [a-zA-Z]+\s*=.*require\(['\"].*['\"]\);$" src/ | xargs -I {} sed -i -r "s/^let ([a-zA-Z]+)\s*=.*require\(['\"]([^'\"]*)['\"]\);$/import \1 from '\2';/g" {}

# For `require('xxx');`
ag -l "^require\(['\"].*['\"]\);$" src/ | xargs -I {} sed -i -r "s/^require\(['\"]([^'\"]*)['\"]\);$/import '\1';/g" {}
```
### style(export): Use export default instead of module.exports …

``` sh
ag -l 'module.exports =' index.js src | xargs -I {} sed -i -r 's/^module.exports =/export default/g' {}
```

Special treatment for `src/lib/version.js` which is generated and
used in `scripts`, hence the update of
`scripts/bump-package-version.js`.
### Notes

As you might have noticed, the linter is now only passed on `index.js` and `src`. Scripts shouldn't rely on `node` understanding the ES6 syntax yet.
This has one drawback though : your editor will be screaming while editing js files in `scripts`. Not sure how to handle that. Maybe exclude them using `.eslintrc` ?

Also, I'm still unable to run `npm shrinkwrap`, hence the failing tests.
